reviews.review_msg: Prof McAllister leaves no room for debate.  She seems to actively recruiting people for her causes instead of providing a well rounded learning structure.  I was very disappointed in this class.  I don't feel she would be open to hearing my opinion on the matter and actually fear retribution from her.

The course is primarily taught through PowerPoint lectures, and limited materials are posted on Canvas. Although CMNS 202 is a 200-level course, it includes several writing assignments that require museum visits, which may feel demanding for some students.

This review is for CMNS 202 but it wasn't an option to put in. She's very disorganized (kept pushing back deadlines) and late to lecture constantly. Horrible lecturer too, literally repeats the same thing over and over again, you're not learning anything in her class. The assignments were boring and tedious. Take 202 with anyone else for your sake.

Kirsten is a great professor. Definitely reading heavy course but if you're attending lectures, you're totally fine.

She is accessible outside class and so understanding. Her grading is fair and she provides amazing feedback that actually showcases how to do better and she gives further opportunities to work for better grades.

Dr McAllister is one of the sweetest professors I've come across in my 4 years. She's so caring and genuine and makes sure the students are engaging in class and not left out. The readings are heavy but actively listening to her lectures and examples help alot. Kirsten also engages in a lot of group interaction among students that makes things easy

One of the worst classes I have taken so far, she doesn't provide any slides for her students to look back at, she expects to take notes of every word. There are no assignment guidelines available in canvas. Boring lectures and boring instructor.

Worst professor I've had in my 4 years. Her lectures were confusing, jumping between unrelated concepts that were too abstract. Assignment details and lecture slides weren't on Canvas, and pop quizzes made no sense. The final exam took 4 hours and we also have a final assignment too. This class made no sense and had no connection to CMNS.

This was the most unorganized class I have ever taken. She posts no instructions for any assignments or even lecture slides on Canvas. The assignments are so confusing and horribly explained. The final exam was a complete joke. Instructions for any assignment / exam have so much detail that you get lost. I would not recommend taking her classes.

Kirsten is one of the worst professors I have ever had at SFU. She struggles to make anything in her class interesting, in every lecture, she fixates on minor details instead of covering the material. My favourite thing about Kirsten is she tells us to email her if we have questions but then never responds. Don't take any of her classes.

Kristen is one of the worst most unorganized professors I've had in my last 4 years at SFU. Do not trust the old reviews that gave her a 5/5 Idk what happened between 2014 and now but she is a horrible prof that forces you to come to class with pop quizzes and on paper assignments (if she didn't no one would come to her repetitive, boring classes).

The most disorganized and confusing class I've ever taken. Not just me complaining but everyone is complaining in the group chat. The lecture is long and dried and the TA is absolutely helpless which makes things even more confusing. There are three exams throughout the term and each requires you to write 2100 words. It's just crazy.

One of the worst profsclasses I have taken at SFU. The most confusing person to follow, whether it's lectures or assignment expectations. She got so upset that students kept leaving her AWFUL lectures that she started telling TA's to mark harder & stopped providing lecture notes, which were needed for assignments. Just awful, -310 would recommend

just awful. seriously awful. I would never take a class with her again. Terrible formatting no clarity on assignments whatsoever. I'm still lost on what assignment #2 was about (archive or semiotics). Why is this class even required I didn't learn anything.

Kirsten was the best prof i've had in my four years at SFU. She is extremely passionate about photography and her area of research which made her lectures even more interesting. She used examples from her research as well and had a guest speaker come in. The topic was very relevant and useful and made you examine photographs more critically.
Very good class!! Love her so much. I can't wait to take another class with her.

Great professor; fair marker; to do fair in the course, you must do the readings and participate to do well; really cares about students and her office hours are dedicated to actually helping students improve your essay; very enthusiastic about the course; recommended :)

Hands down one of the best profs/courses that I've ever had. Took this class few years back but time and time again I've come back to the concepts I learned and articles I read for this course. She is a very serious researcher/lecturer but I was blown away by how helpful she can be as long as you seek it on reasonable terms.

Kirsten is such a kind and awesome teacher, she will go above and beyond to help her students out. I have never had a prof spend so much time helping.  Anyone who wrote a bad comment, clearly did not ask her for help and just complained. She does have very detailed assignments and this is not a slack course. My TA was awesome as well.

Super interesting class as long as you bring your brain, best if you have opinions. Kirsten is a serious researcher so she can be a bit strict in marking papers, but she gives clear instructions of how to do well. Very lenient on deadlines but assignments can be time-consuming from real ethnographic work.
Best Prof I've had in university. Informative, passionate lectures. Very demanding with assignment expectations, but I learned so much.
Very kind, and great assignments, however, lectures we slow and her notes were hard to follow. Overall enjoyed the course.

Kirsten is a great prof. I learned alot from her class and ended up enjoying it so much more than I expected. There is ALOT of reading and you need to use them for the assignments. She has wonderful TAs - I'd take a class with her again!

Went back to her themes&just repeated them. some lectures were more of an idea - ie AUTHORITY, and werent explored too well. she used low-brow cliches about people to prove her point. I found her sense of humur annoying; she's friendly tho. TA Ayaka was very helpful tho and our tutrl's stimulating & focused. hwk easy enough and fun, got me excited!

The material makes sense but the approach is to sporadic to follow. The notes are barely comprehensible, and McAllister likes to make up her own vocab, but the course is valuable and the assignments are enjoyable.

Kirsten a warm and very approachable instructor. Her lectures are dull, and her notes are not precise. However, this doesn't impede understanding of the material. Conversely, the assignments are fascinating and really well thought out. As it is a 'research methods' course, you will be stunned by what you learn when you apply the instructed methods.

I had expected the material in this class to be dry, but Dr. McAllister really shakes it up and makes things interesting. She is articulate and thoughtful, so I am hoping to take more courses with her in the future.

262 could have been super dull, but it was way more interesting than i expected. She's very approachable for extra help/clarification, and is a good resource if you're interested in doing research down the road. As for hotness - it's a stupid category , but for what it's worth, i think she's one of the best looking folks in the CMNS department.

Dr. McAllister is a really dynamic, interesting prof, esp in her smaller, senior classes. I did her intro 262 mega-class last yr & that was a waste of her talent. She is one of the top profs i've ever had - brilliant, with an international reputation. Like all newer profs, she's a bit uptight, but we call her Dr. M. and she relaxes.
It is dull material, but Kirsten makes it about at interesting as it can get:)

Terribly boring lecturer. I would never take another class from her. Her assignments left no room for any creative thinking (she's very controlling). She might as well have just written the papers because she was only open to hearing her own opinion. Did not learn anything interesting in her class....Torturous attending her lecutures.

I actually found kirsten really helpful and her lectures were always organized. Assignments are not very hard and quite straight foward.  She was very clear in what she wanted from her students. I find her more helpful than her TA because she clarifies concepts. The first half of the course is quite dry which is why some may not enjoy it.

I think she means well, and she is very nice, but seriously dull lectures.  I didn't learn much from lecture, and found most of the notes not useful for assignments.  If I didn't have an awesome T.A. I would have been lost.  I can't believe I spent ~$60 per class for those lectures.

Where to begin? Her lectures are uninformative, and super disorganized. She makes snarky comments during lecture and her assignments could not be less clear if they were written in pig latin. Actually they might make more sense that way. the only saving grace that I had was my TA who almost made up for her complete lack of teaching ability.

I'm blown away by what an immensely inept lecturer McAllister is. The class is a complete waste of time & money. Yes, she means well but really, she/we should be put out of her teaching misery.  Possibly the most useless instructor I've ever had. Her examples are terrible & she has the intellectual vigour of a housefly. I've learned zilch from her.
she gives very clear,well-organized and straight-forward lecs.i dunno y ppl put such harsh marks for her.i didn't get dat great mark :( but this course was veeeeeeeeeeeery useful

I think McAllister is the perfect example of a teacher who "means well" but is in way over her head. She has a fun personality, but as time goes on it becomes clear she has no clue how to give a lecture. A nice woman, but she really gives off a vibe of being ditzy and un-coordinated. Dumb, even.
Amateurish, nervous, disorganized. With the tuition costs we're paying, we deserve professional instruction - dump her.

Easy course, though the material is a bit boring. Needs to work on lecturing skills b/c she tends to be repetitive and oversimplify concepts. Midterms and assignments are easy as long as you do all the readings and pay attn to her notes.

Ya she's hot. SO put together. Articulate, inspiring, brilliant, hilarious.  The other day I went into her office and let the cat out of the bag about being stressed out about another course and she sat me down and gave me the best pep talk ever. Some teachers know how to set the bar high.

I took an upper div course with her that is right up her alley, so her teaching was TOP shape. I find her an excellent educator, and very helpful for extra help. And hey, she may not be your type, but I think she is pretty cute.

She's my prof in an upper div, seminar-type class, and I think she's doing a very good job.  I like her materials, they're reasonably challenging and useful for developing new perspectives.  She's also helpful and approachable; teaching style could be better, but overall, a good prof.

her teaching style needs serious adjustment.  I must say that her instructions for assignments are poor.    Her overhead notes are not a good guideline because she jumps back and forth from topic to topic.  If you decide to take the course make sure to be self-organized.

Kirsten is a great prof, I do appreciate that she was not particularily great in the lower div lecture, but I think it was the material as well as her other clas she was teaching at the time.As a seminar prof she is great. Also she is really easy going.
i think that the fact that she taught a history course should be taken into account. she may not have been entertaining (usually hist courses aren't) but i learned a lot of info that i can use later.

Wow... very very boring prof. My goodness. I fell asleep half the time, and didn't bother coming 1/4 of the time, leaving me only 1/4 of the time actually there and somewhat attentive. Her movies were cool though. But... yeah... boring. Also, read above.

I hate the class mostly because Kirsten is so confusing and repetitive. she doesnt take enough time for the larger topics. if you need help talk to the TA because she will make your problems worse. i cant stand her.

Her lectures are horribly slow and excruciating.  I have fallen asleep during them.  She reads off of her computer half of the time and fails to address the students in a effective manner.  Ironic, considering this is a COMMUNICATIONS course.
